mission.title: fluentd: CI: Some tests about signal handlers sometimes fail
mission.description: CI: Some tests about signal handlers sometimes fail. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current fluentd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your fluentd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
helm list -n fluentd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working fluentd installation.

steps.title: Review fluentd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluentd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
kubectl get configmap -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluentd
```
### Describe the bug

`test_cont_in_main_process_signal_handlers` and `test_cont_in_supervisor_signal_handler` of `SupervisorTest` sometimes fail.

steps.title: Apply the fix for CI: Some tests about signal handlers sometimes fail
steps.description:
If it use the object applied `dont_allow` in the other test through `ObjectSpace.each_object`, it will cause the error.
To relax object constraints, this patch will use `never` instead.

See sample code in https://github.com/fluent/fluentd/issues/4063#issuecomment-2416121325
```yaml
===============================================================================
Failure: test_cont_in_main_process_signal_handlers(SupervisorTest):
  class()
  Called 0 times.
  Expected 1 times.
/opt/hostedtoolcache/Ruby/3.0.5/x64/lib/ruby/gems/3.0.0/gems/sigdump-0.2.4/lib/sigdump.rb:74:in `block in dump_object_count'
/opt/hostedtoolcache/Ruby/3.0.5/x64/lib/ruby/gems/3.0.0/gems/sigdump-0.2.4/lib/sigdump.rb:73:in `each_object'
/opt/hostedtoolcache/Ruby/3.0.5/x64/lib/ruby/gems/3.0.0/gems/sigdump-0.2.4/lib/sigdump.rb:73:in `dump_object_count'
/opt/hostedtoolcache/Ruby/3.0.5/x64/lib/ruby/gems/3.0
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
kubectl get events -n fluentd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "CI: Some tests about signal handlers sometimes fail" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
If it use the object applied `dont_allow` in the other test through `ObjectSpace.each_object`, it will cause the error.
To relax object constraints, this patch will use `never` instead.

See sample code in https://github.com/fluent/fluentd/issues/4063#issuecomment-2416121325
codeSnippets:
===============================================================================
Failure: test_cont_in_main_process_signal_handlers(SupervisorTest):
  class()
  Called 0 times.
  Expected 1 times.
/opt/hostedtoolcache/Ruby/3.0.5/x64/lib/ruby/gems/3.0.0/gems/sigdump-0.2.4/lib/sigdump.rb:74:in `block in dump_object_count'
/opt/hostedtoolcache/Ruby/3.0.5/x64/lib/ruby/gems/3.0.0/gems/sigdump-0.2.4/lib/sigdump.rb:73:in `each_object'
/opt/hostedtoolcache/Ruby/3.0.5/x64/lib/ruby/gems/3.0.0/gems/sigdump-0.2.4/lib/sigdump.rb:73:in `dump_object_count'
/opt/hostedtoolcache/Ruby/3.0.5/x64/lib/ruby/gems/3.0.0/gems/sigdump-0.2.4/lib/sigdump.rb:18:in `block in dump'
/opt/hostedtoolcache/Ruby/3.0.5/x64/lib/ruby/gems/3.0.0/gems/sigdump-0.2.4/lib/sigdump.rb:136:in `open'
/opt/hostedtoolcache/Ruby/3.0.5/x64/l
Looks like that `mock(Process).kill(:WINCH, -1).never` causes this error.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluentd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
